dimensions.name: Curriculum coherence & progression
dimensions.justification: The 6-term plan is purpose-built and coherent for pre-Physician-Assistant preparation: the biology foundations (BIOL 200 -> BIOL 201/202 and BIOL 205/206) feed directly into the clinical-prep sequence PA programs require, with BIOL 340 Mammalian Physiology, BIOL 421 Comparative Vertebrate Anatomy, R120 335 General Microbiology, CHEM 473 Biochemistry, and PSY 210 Introduction to Psychology all placed deliberately, and the chemistry/physics support courses ordered underneath. The compression into three years is aggressive but the sequencing itself is logical.
dimensions.suggestion: Flag the front-loaded depth (e.g., BIOL 340 Mammalian Physiology in the second year) with an advising note so students understand the accelerated pacing before they commit.

dimensions.name: Prerequisite structure health
dimensions.justification: The prerequisite graph is sound: deepestPrereqChain=7 reflects genuine build-up (foundations -> physiology/anatomy -> advanced courses), which is expected in a compressed accelerated track, and the 8 dangling references (BIOL 202, BIOL 206, CHEM 125A, CHEM 126A, GER 300, HUM 099, PHYS 102A, PHYS 103A) are co-requisite labs, a developmental writing placeholder, and GER placeholders rather than real breaks.
dimensions.suggestion: Resolve the HUM 099 and co-requisite-lab references so the accelerated chain has no dangling nodes that could confuse tight scheduling.

dimensions.name: Learning outcomes & objectives
dimensions.justification: No program-level learning outcomes or objectives appear in the data, which is a notable omission for a selective accelerated professional pathway where the competencies leading into PA study should be explicit.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish learning outcomes tied to PA-school readiness (clinical science foundations, quantitative literacy, and professional competencies).

dimensions.name: Credit distribution & balance
dimensions.justification: The plan reconciles exactly (statedTotalCredits=102, summedTermCredits=102, creditReconcileDelta=0). The 102-credit total is a deliberate feature of the accelerated combined structure (undergraduate portion feeding the PA program) rather than a shortfall, and the distribution is appropriately science-dense with limited free electives.
dimensions.suggestion: Add a one-line note that 102 credits reflect the undergraduate portion of the combined B.A./PA pathway so the below-120 total is not misread as incomplete.

dimensions.name: Presentation & clarity
dimensions.justification: Terms are labeled by year and semester and credits total to 102. Clarity is only lightly reduced by course rows that reference legacy codes without a resolvable code ('R120 335 General Microbiology', 'R120 360 Biochemistry') and by null-code 'or' alternative rows.
dimensions.suggestion: Give the R120-referenced requirements (General Microbiology, Molecular Biology/Biochemistry) resolvable current course codes so every plan row links to a real catalog entry.

dimensions.name: Industry & currency relevance
dimensions.justification: The curriculum is well aligned with current clinical-science preparation: BIOL 340 Mammalian Physiology, BIOL 421 Comparative Vertebrate Anatomy, R120 335 General Microbiology, BIOL 350 Immunology (available), CHEM 473 Biochemistry, and PSY 210 Introduction to Psychology cover the physiology/anatomy/behavioral base PA programs expect. Gaps relative to modern clinical practice are limited quantitative training (only MATH 105) and no explicit epidemiology, pharmacology, or healthcare-ethics content.
dimensions.suggestion: Add an epidemiology/biostatistics course and a healthcare-ethics or professionalism component to match competencies emphasized in current PA education.

descriptionQuality.justification: Descriptions are substantive across the clinical-prep spine (BIOL 340 Mammalian Physiology, BIOL 421 Comparative Vertebrate Anatomy, BIOL 350 Immunology, CHEM 473 Biochemistry), with only isolated gaps such as the empty excerpt for BIOL 352 Genetics.

prerequisiteClarity.justification: Prerequisites carry explicit grade conditions, but clarity is reduced by legacy dual-numbering ('BIOL 201 and BIOL 202 or R120 201 and R120 202') and by plan rows that reference R120 courses (R120 335, R120 360) without a resolvable current code, which is risky in a tightly sequenced accelerated program.

missingTopics.rationale: Quantitative preparation is limited to MATH 105 Elementary Probability and Statistics; PA study and evidence-based practice require stronger epidemiology/statistics.
The plan builds strong normal-physiology coverage (BIOL 340) but no pharmacology or systematic pathophysiology course prepares students for clinical reasoning.
A selective clinical pathway should include explicit ethics/professionalism content; none appears alongside the science core.
With immunology (BIOL 350) and microbiology (R120 335) present, a population-health or global-health course would round out the clinical foundation.

emergingTrends.trend: Health informatics and electronic health records
emergingTrends.rationale: BNFO 135 programming is already required; extending it toward clinical data and EHR literacy matches how modern PAs practice.
Clinical delivery is shifting to remote and digital modalities, which the current physiology/anatomy-focused plan does not address.
PA competencies increasingly emphasize team-based practice, a theme absent from the individual-course clinical foundation.
Strengthening statistics/epidemiology beyond MATH 105 would prepare students for the evidence-based, data-heavy clinical environment they are entering.

topSuggestions: Publish learning outcomes tied to PA-school readiness; none currently appear.
Add epidemiology/biostatistics beyond MATH 105 and introduce pharmacology/pathophysiology to align with current PA competencies.
Give the R120-referenced plan rows (General Microbiology, Biochemistry/Molecular Biology) resolvable current course codes.
Add a note clarifying that the 102-credit total reflects the undergraduate portion of the combined accelerated pathway.
Add a healthcare-ethics/professionalism component to complement the strong clinical-science core.
